race.name: Peaks and Plains
race.tagline: Unsupported ultra-distance loop over the Harz's highest passes

vitals.start_format: Mass start, solo or 2-person pair categories, GPS tracker mandatory
vitals.registration: Online via race|result (my.raceresult.com); 2026 places sold out in ~2 minutes; 2027 switched to a lottery
vitals.feed_zones: None — fully unsupported; riders carry or source their own food/water

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 59. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 56.

course_description.character: A self-navigated, unsupported ultra-distance loop out of Magdeburg: riders cross the flat Magdeburger Börde to reach the Harz, then zigzag across the range's highest peaks and passes — including the Brocken, the Harz's tallest summit — before returning across the plains. Riders follow a prescribed GPX route solo or in registered pairs, carry live GPS trackers, and get no feed zones, mechanical support, or marshaled traffic control; the clock (48 hours) and the route file are the only structure. Published 2026 rider accounts (a finisher and the winner) separately reported storms, heat, punctures, navigation-equipment failure, and repeated mechanicals during the race, on top of the climbing itself.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Brocken and a claimed circuit of the ten highest Harz passes, tackled solo or in a pair with zero outside support across one or two overnight stretches.
suffering_zones: Brocken ascent — the Harz's highest summit, the marquee climb of the route
The Harz pass circuit — repeated short, steep climbs back-to-back with no feed support between them
Overnight Börde crossings — flat but exposed return legs where fatigue and navigation errors (GPS/route-file failures were reported in 2026) compound

logistics.airport: Berlin Brandenburg (BER) or Hannover (HAJ) are the nearest major airports, each reachable from Magdeburg by road/rail
logistics.lodging_strategy: Self-arranged; unsupported event, plan overnight stays in Magdeburg or en route as needed within the 48-hour window.
logistics.food: None provided on course — fully unsupported; riders must carry or self-source all food and water.

history.origin_story: Organized by freshpepper GmbH & Co. KG, a Magdeburg event company (managing director Martin Hummelt) that ran the regional CYCLE TOUR road-cycling program for a decade before ending it in 2025; Peaks & Plains launched in 2024 (initially ~500km/6,000m) as one of the organizer's continuing focus projects and has run annually since (2024, 2025, 2026).
notable_moments: 2026: Sebastian Trimborn reported as first rider back into Magdeburg
2026: registration sold out in roughly 2 minutes, prompting the organizer to switch 2027 entry to a lottery
history.reputation: Young but growing fast — demand for the ~150 available 2026 spots outstripped supply badly enough that the organizer moved to a lottery system for 2027.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real, Young, In-Demand Unsupported Ultra
biased_opinion.summary: Peaks & Plains is exactly what it advertises: a timed, competitive, fully unsupported ultra-distance loop through the Harz's highest passes, run by an established German event organizer (freshpepper, ex-CYCLE TOUR) rather than an ad-hoc grassroots effort. It's genuinely competitive — GPS-tracked, disqualifiable for shortcuts or outside assistance, with recorded placings and a named 2026 winner — not a brevet-style completion ride. Only three editions in (2024-2026), it's already outgrown its own field size: 2026 places vanished in about two minutes, and the organizer is moving to a lottery for 2027. That's a good problem for the event but a bad one for anyone hoping to just sign up.
strengths: Legitimate, competitive ultra format — GPS trackers, no-drafting rules outside pairs, recorded placings and a named winner, not a self-reported brevet
Run by an experienced organizer (freshpepper, a decade of CYCLE TOUR behind it) rather than a first-time grassroots operation
Genuine Harz highlights — the Brocken and a claimed circuit of the range's ten highest passes — on a route that's clearly drawn real, credible rider reports of tough conditions
Real demand signal: sold out in ~2 minutes in 2026, which is a meaningfully different problem than most Tier 3 events in this database have
weaknesses: Only 3 editions old (debuted 2024) — no long track record yet on organization consistency year-to-year
Small field (~150 in 2026) that already can't meet demand — expect a 2027 lottery, not a guaranteed entry, even if you want in early

Zero support on course (no feed zones, no mechanical support) raises the self-sufficiency bar well above a typical gran fondo — this is closer to an unsupported ultra-race than the mixed-terrain gran fondo framing the database's dimension set assumes

Advertised course specs (555km/7,300m) run slightly ahead of what post-race rider and press accounts actually measured (~550km/7,200m) — close enough to not matter for planning, but worth knowing the advertised numbers are a touch generous
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a real, competitively-timed, unsupported ultra through some of the Harz's best climbing and you can get a spot (increasingly a lottery, not a sign-up), Peaks & Plains delivers. It is not a supported gran fondo — go in ready to navigate, fix mechanicals, and feed yourself for up to 48 hours solo or with one partner.

final_verdict.one_liner: Unsupported road ultra through Harz peaks offers epic climbing and scenery.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Race it if you want a legitimate, GPS-timed, self-supported ultra-distance test through real Harz mountain terrain and can handle solo/pair logistics with zero course support. Skip it if you want a marshaled, fed, supported gran fondo experience — this is an ultra-race, not a sportive, despite living in a gran-fondo-labeled database.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similarly self-supported German/Alpine ultra format, look at other freshpepper-adjacent or Rad-Race-style ultracycling events; for the same Harz climbing with full event support, look for a traditional marshaled Harz-region gran fondo or sportive instead.

citations.label: Official Imprint — freshpepper GmbH & Co. KG, Martin Hummelt
r/ultracycling — 2026 Registration Sold Out in ~2 Minutes
CYCLE TOUR — Organizer History, 10 Years + Peaks & Plains Continuation

eligibility.notes: Third annual edition held 2026 (debuted 2024); organizer freshpepper GmbH & Co. KG already announced a May 2027 edition. 2026 field (~150 riders) sold out in ~2 minutes, prompting a switch to lottery-based entry for 2027 — expect limited/lottery access, not open registration.
